behaviors.description: The generated reply references specific points from the incoming email rather than producing a generic response.
behaviors.justification: This is a response-quality/grounding claim about LLM output. A deterministic mock can pass while real model output is generic or misses thread details. The failure boundary is the real model’s non-deterministic generation behavior, not just local prompt wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Run with a real LLM on a set of real/sanitized email threads containing unique, checkable points (dates, decisions, asks). Assert each draft cites thread-specific points (not generic boilerplate) using a rubric-based evaluator and spot checks over repeated runs.

behaviors.description: The factual consistency check flags claims in the draft that contradict entries in the knowledge base.
behaviors.justification: Contradiction detection is a classification-quality task. If this checker uses LLM/model judgment, mocks or deterministic stubs can hide real misses/false negatives. The key failure is model reasoning against KB facts.
behaviors.test_description: Feed drafts with seeded contradictory claims against KB entries into the real consistency checker (with real model call if used). Verify contradictions are flagged with evidence links; include near-miss cases to detect over/under-flagging.

behaviors.description: When the knowledge base has no relevant entries, the draft includes a disclaimer rather than fabricating facts.
behaviors.justification: Even if retrieval-empty handling is wired locally, the critical risk is the model fabricating details when context is missing. Mocked generation can miss this. Correctness depends on real LLM behavior under missing-knowledge conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Use threads where KB retrieval returns no relevant documents, then generate drafts through the real model. Assert the draft contains the required uncertainty/disclaimer language and does not introduce unsupported factual claims across repeated trials.

behaviors.description: The approval workflow blocks sending until the user explicitly approves — no draft is sent automatically.
behaviors.justification: The real failure boundary is cross-module state handoff (draft state, approval event, send service). A unit test of one guard function can miss orchestration/race mistakes. No real external API is required to catch the core failure.
behaviors.test_description: Wire controller/service/repository with a local fake mail sender. Create draft, attempt send without approval, assert zero send attempts and blocked status. Then submit explicit approval event and assert exactly one send transition.

behaviors.description: The full sequence — read thread, generate draft, check facts, present for approval — works end-to-end for a real email thread.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly a multi-step pipeline including agentic generation and agentic/dynamic fact checking, plus gating before send. Single-step Agentic or System tests can miss sequencing/state-carry failures across steps.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the production-like pipeline on a real email thread: ingest thread, generate via real LLM, run real fact-check step against KB, present approval UI/state, and verify no send before approval and successful send after approval. Validate artifacts and transitions at each stage.
